sections.label: Section 835
sections.original: βεβάκχευται μανίαις ,
sections.translation: and now is raving mad , a prey to the Furies , whirling blood with racing eyes , the son of Agamemnon ! O the wretch ! when

sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: χρυσεοπηνήτων φαρέων
sections.translation: he saw a mother ’ s bosom over her robe of golden weave , and yet he made her his victim , in recompense for his father ’ s sufferings .

sections.label: Section 844
sections.original: γυναῖκες , ἦ που τῶνδʼ ἀφώρμηται δόμων
sections.translation: Women , has my poor Orestes left the house ,

sections.label: Section 845
sections.original: τλήμων Ὀρέστης θεομανεῖ λύσσῃ δαμείς ;
sections.translation: mastered by the heaven-sent madness ?

sections.label: Section 846
sections.original: ἥκιστα · πρὸς δʼ Ἀργεῖον οἴχεται λεών ,
sections.translation: Not at all ; he has gone to the Argive people to stand the appointed trial for his life , in which he and you must live or die .

sections.label: Section 849
sections.original: οἴμοι · τί χρῆμʼ ἔδρασε ; τίς δʼ ἔπεισέ νιν ;
sections.translation: Oh ! Why did he do it ? Who persuaded him ?

sections.label: Section 850
sections.original: Πυλάδης · ἔοικε δʼ οὐ μακρὰν ὅδʼ ἄγγελος
sections.translation: Pylades ; but this messenger will no doubt soon tell us what happened to your brother there .

sections.label: Section 852
sections.original: ὦ τλῆμον , ὦ δύστηνε τοῦ στρατηλάτου
sections.translation: Wretched , unhappy daughter of the general Agamemnon , my lady Electra , hear the sad tidings I bring you .

sections.label: Section 855
sections.original: αἰαῖ , διοιχόμεσθα · δῆλος εἶ λόγῳ .
sections.translation: Alas ! we are ruined ; your words show it ; you have clearly come with tidings of woe .

sections.label: Section 857
sections.original: ψήφῳ Πελασγῶν σὸν κασίγνητον θανεῖν
sections.translation: The Pelasgians have decided by vote that you , poor lady , and your brother are to die this day .

sections.label: Section 859
sections.original: οἴμοι · προσῆλθεν ἐλπίς , ἣν φοβουμένη
sections.translation: Alas ! my expectations are realized ; I have long feared this ,

sections.label: Section 860
sections.original: πάλαι τὸ μέλλον ἐξετηκόμην γόοις .
sections.translation: and have been wasting away in mourning for what was sure to happen . But what was the trial , what was said by the Argives , to condemn us and ratify our death ? Tell me , old friend ; must I die by stoning or the sword ?

sections.label: Section 865
sections.original: κοινὰς ἀδελφῷ συμφορὰς κεκτημένην ;
sections.translation: For I share my brother ’ s misfortunes .

sections.label: Section 866
sections.original: ἐτύγχανον μὲν ἀγρόθεν πυλῶν ἔσω
sections.translation: I had just come from the country and was entering the gates , needing to learn what was decided about you and Orestes , for I was always well disposed to your father when he was alive , and it was your house that reared me ,

sections.label: Section 870
sections.original: πένητα μέν , χρῆσθαι δὲ γενναῖον φίλοις .
sections.translation: poor indeed , yet loyal in the service of friends . I saw a crowd going and taking their seats on the height , where they say Danaus first gathered his people for a meeting , making amends to Aegyptus . So , when I saw the throng , I asked a citizen :

sections.label: Section 875
sections.original: Τί καινὸν Ἄργει ; μῶν τι πολεμίων πάρα
sections.translation: What news in Argos ? Tidings of the enemy haven ’ t ruffled the city of Danaus , have they ? But he said : Don ’ t you see Orestes there , on his way to he tried for his life ? I saw an unexpected sight , which I wish I had not seen ,

sections.label: Section 880
sections.original: Πυλάδην τε καὶ σὸν σύγγονον στείχονθʼ ὁμοῦ ,
sections.translation: Pylades and your brother approaching together , the one with his head down , weakened by sickness ; the other sharing his friend ’ s sorrow like a brother , tending his illness with constant care .

sections.label: Section 884
sections.original: ἐπεὶ δὲ πλήρης ἐγένετʼ Ἀργείων ὄχλος ,
sections.translation: Now when the Argives were fully gathered ,

sections.label: Section 885
sections.original: κῆρυξ ἀναστὰς εἶπε · Τίς χρῄζει λέγειν ,
sections.translation: a herald rose and said : Who wishes to give his opinion whether Orestes should be slain or not for the murder of his mother ? Then up stood Talthybius , who helped your father sack the Phrygians . He spoke out of both sides of his mouth , a mere tool of those in power as he always is ,

sections.label: Section 890
sections.original: διχόμυθα , πατέρα μὲν σὸν ἐκπαγλούμενος ,
sections.translation: expressing high admiration for your father , but not praising your brother , urging his crooked sentiments in specious words , that it would establish laws as to parents that are not good ; and all the while he was darting lively glances at the friends of Aegisthus .

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: τὸ γὰρ γένος τοιοῦτον · ἐπὶ τὸν εὐτυχῆ
sections.translation: Such is that tribe ; heralds always trip across to the lucky side ; the one who has power in the city or a post in the government is their friend .

sections.label: Section 898
sections.original: ἐπὶ τῷδε δʼ ἠγόρευε Διομήδης ἄναξ .
sections.translation: After him lord Diomedes made a speech ; he said they should not kill you and your brother ,

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: εἴα , φυγῇ δὲ ζημιοῦντας εὐσεβεῖν .
sections.translation: but keep clear of guilt by punishing you with exile . Some roared out that his words were good , but others disapproved .

sections.label: Section 902
sections.original: οἳ δʼ οὐκ ἐπῄνουν . κἀπὶ τῷδʼ ἀνίσταται
sections.translation: Next stood up a fellow , who cannot close his lips ; one whose impudence is his strength ; an Argive , but not of Argos , forced on us ;

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: θορύβῳ τε πίσυνος κἀμαθεῖ παρρησίᾳ ,
sections.translation: confident in bluster and ignorant free speech , and plausible enough to involve them in some mischief sooner or later ; for whenever a man with a pleasing trick of speech , but of unsound principles , persuades the mob , it is a serious evil to the state ; but those who give sound and sensible advice on all occasions ,

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: κἂν μὴ παραυτίκʼ , αὖθίς εἰσι χρήσιμοι
sections.translation: if not immediately useful to the state , yet prove so afterwards . And this is the way in which to regard a party leader ; for the position is much the same in the case of an orator and a man in office . He was for stoning you and Orestes to death ,

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: βάλλοντας · ὑπὸ δʼ ἔτεινε Τυνδάρεως λόγους
sections.translation: but it was Tyndareus who kept suggesting arguments of this kind to him as he urged the death of both of you .

sections.label: Section 917
sections.original: ἄλλος δʼ ἀναστὰς ἔλεγε τῷδʼ ἐναντία ,
sections.translation: Another then stood up and said the opposite ; he was not handsome in appearance , but a brave man , rarely coming in contact with the town or the circle in the market-place ;

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: αὐτουργός — οἵπερ καὶ μόνοι σῴζουσι γῆν —
sections.translation: a farmer — and they are the only ones who preserve our land — but clever , and eager to grapple with the arguments , his character without a blemish , his walk in life beyond reproach . He said that they should crown Orestes , the son of Agamemnon , for showing his willingness to avenge a father

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: κακὴν γυναῖκα κἄθεον κατακτανών ,
sections.translation: by the murder of a wicked and godless woman who would prevent men from taking up arms and going on foreign service , if those who remain behind , corrupt and seduce wives left at home to keep house .

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: καὶ τοῖς γε χρηστοῖς εὖ λέγειν ἐφαίνετο .
sections.translation: To the better sort , at least , his word carried conviction .

sections.label: Section 931
sections.original: κοὐδεὶς ἔτʼ εἶπε . σὸς δʼ ἐπῆλθε σύγγονος ,
sections.translation: No one spoke after him . Then your brother came forward and said : You dwellers in the land of Inachus ! Pelasgians in ancient times , and later Danaids I helped you no less than my father

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: ἔκτεινα μητέρʼ . εἰ γὰρ ἀρσένων φόνος
sections.translation: when I slew my mother ; for if the murder of men by women is to be sanctioned , then the sooner you die , the better , or you must become the slaves of women ; and that will be doing the very reverse of what you should . As it is , she who betrayed my father ’ s bed

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: τέθνηκεν · εἰ δὲ δὴ κατακτενεῖτʼ ἐμέ ,
sections.translation: has died , but if you take my life , the law becomes relaxed , and the sooner each one of you dies , the better ; for it will never be daring at any rate that they will lack . Yet , for all he seemed to speak well , he did not persuade the assembly ; but that villain who spoke in favor of slaying you and your brother

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: ὃς ἠγόρευσε σύγγονον σέ τε κτανεῖν .
sections.translation: gained his point by appealing to the mob .

sections.label: Section 946
sections.original: μόλις δʼ ἔπεισε μὴ πετρουμένους θανεῖν
sections.translation: Poor Orestes scarcely persuaded them not to kill him by stoning , promising to die by his own hand , with you , on this day . Pylades , in tears , is now bringing him from the conclave ;

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: Πυλάδης δακρύων · σὺν δʼ ὁμαρτοῦσιν φίλοι
sections.translation: and his friends bear him company , with wailing and lamentation ; so he comes , a bitter sight and piteous vision . Make ready the sword or prepare the noose for your neck , for you must leave the light ; your noble birth

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: οὐδέν σʼ ἐπωφέλησεν , οὐδʼ ὁ Πύθιος
sections.translation: availed you nothing , nor did Phoebus from his seat on the tripod at Delphi ; he was your undoing . Exit Messenger .

sections.label: Section 957
sections.original: ὦ δυστάλαινα παρθένʼ , ὡς ξυνηρεφὲς
sections.translation: Ah , hapless maiden ! How silent you are , your face covered and bent to the ground , as if about to dash upon a course of lamentation and wailing .

sections.label: Section 1.960
sections.original: κατάρχομαι στεναγμόν , ὦ Πελασγία ,
sections.translation: O Pelasgia , I take up the dirge , doing bloody outrage on my cheeks with white nail , and beating on my head ; these are the portion of Persephone , fair young goddess of the nether world .

sections.label: Section 1.965
sections.original: ἰαχείτω δὲ γᾶ Κυκλωπία ,
sections.translation: Let the Cyclopian land break forth into wailing for the sorrows of our house , laying the steel upon the head to crop it close . This is the piteous , piteous strain that goes up for those who are about to die ,

sections.label: Section 1.970
sections.original: στρατηλατᾶν Ἑλλάδος ποτʼ ὄντων .
sections.translation: once the battle-leaders of Hellas .

sections.label: Section 1.971
sections.original: βέβακε γὰρ βέβακεν , οἴχεται τέκνων
sections.translation: It has gone , it has gone , and is lost , all the race of Pelops , and the glory that crowned their happy home once ; the envy of heaven seized them and that cruel

sections.label: Section 1.975
sections.original: φοινία ψῆφος ἐν πολίταις .
sections.translation: murdering vote among the citizens .

sections.label: Section 1.976
sections.original: ἰὼ ἰώ , πανδάκρυτʼ ἐφαμέρων
sections.translation: Oh , oh ! you tribes of short-lived men , full of tears , full of suffering , see how fate runs counter to your hopes ! All receive in turn their different

sections.label: Section 1.980
sections.original: πήματʼ ἐν χρόνῳ μακρῷ ·
sections.translation: troubles in length of time ; and the whole of mortal life is uncertain .

sections.label: Section 982
sections.original: μόλοιμι τὰν οὐρανοῦ
sections.translation: Oh ! to reach that rock which hangs suspended midway between earth and heaven , that fragment from Olympus , which swings on chains of gold , so that I may utter my lament

sections.label: Section 985
sections.original: γέροντι πατρὶ Ταντάλῳ
sections.translation: to Tantalus , my forefather , who begot the ancestors of my house . They saw infatuate ruin , the chase of winged steeds , when Pelops in four-horse chariot

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: πελάγεσι διεδίφρευσε , Μυρτίλου φόνον
sections.translation: drove over the sea , hurling the body of murdered Myrtilus into the ocean swell , after his race near Geraestus ’ strand , foam-flecked from the tossing sea .

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: ὅθεν δόμοισι τοῖς ἐμοῖς
sections.translation: From this came a woeful curse upon my house , brought to birth among the sheep by the son of Maia , when there appeared a baleful , baleful portent of a lamb with golden fleece ,

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: Ἀτρέος ἱπποβώτα ·
sections.translation: for Atreus , breeder of horses ; from which Strife changed the course of the sun ’ s winged chariot , fitting the westward path of the sky towards the single horse of Dawn ;

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: ἑπταπόρου τε δράμημα Πελειάδος
sections.translation: and Zeus diverted the career of the seven Pleiads into a new track and exchanged . . . death for death : both the banquet to which Thyestes gave his name , and the treacherous love of Cretan Aerope ,

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: ας δολίοισι γάμοις · τὰ πανύστατα δʼ
sections.translation: in her treacherous marriage ; but the crowning woe has come on me and on my father by the bitter constraints of our house .

sections.label: Section 1013
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ὅδε σὸς σύγγονος ἕρπει
sections.translation: Look , here comes your brother , condemned to die , and with him Pylades , most loyal of friends ,

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: ἰσάδελφος ἀνήρ , ἰθύνων
sections.translation: true as a brother , guiding his feeble steps , his yoke-fellow , pacing carefully .

sections.label: Section 1018
sections.original: οἲ ἐγώ · πρὸ τύμβου γάρ σʼ ὁρῶσʼ ἀναστένω ,
sections.translation: Alas ! I weep to see you stand before the tomb , my brother , face to face with the funeral pyre .

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: οἲ ἐγὼ μάλʼ αὖθις · ὥς σʼ ἰδοῦσʼ ἐν ὄμμασιν
sections.translation: Alas , again ! as I take my last look at you , my senses leave me .

sections.label: Section 1022
sections.original: οὐ σῖγʼ ἀφεῖσα τοὺς γυναικείους γόους
sections.translation: Be silent ! an end to womanish lamenting ! resign yourself to your fate . It is piteous , but nevertheless you must bear the present fate .

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: καὶ πῶς σιωπῶ ; φέγγος εἰσορᾶν θεοῦ
sections.translation: How can I be silent , when we poor sufferers are no longer to gaze upon the sun-god ’ s light ?

sections.label: Section 1027
sections.original: σὺ μή μʼ ἀπόκτεινʼ · ἅλις ὑπʼ Ἀργείας χερὸς
sections.translation: Oh ! spare me that death ! Enough that this unhappy wretch is already slain by Argives ; let our present sufferings be .
